audience: Business owners, marketing managers and project leads preparing or commissioning a new SME website
primaryDutchSearchIntent: KMO's een controleerbare projectchecklist geven voor de voorbereiding, bouw, lancering en overdracht van een nieuwe website

searchIntent.question: What should an SME check when preparing, building, launching and taking ownership of a new website?
searchIntent.geographicContext: Belgium and the wider English-speaking market, with Belgian legal context stated explicitly

longTailKeywords: SEO checklist for a new website
directAnswer: A complete new website checklist covers ownership, scope, site structure, content, SEO, media, accessibility, privacy, testing, launch, handover and ongoing maintenance. Each decision and deliverable should have a named owner, deadline and acceptance criterion.
title: New website checklist: from brief to handover
metaDescription: Planning a new website? Use this complete checklist for goals, content, SEO, media, technology, privacy, testing, launch and handover.
h1: New website checklist: from brief to handover

internalLinks.anchor: complete guide to commissioning a website
how website speed and SEO work together

factsToPreserve: The checklist is a shared project control tool, not a list for one person to complete on launch day
Roles may overlap, but ownership and feedback routes should be explicit
The seven phases run from project framework through handover and growth
SEO begins with search intent, sitemap and page objectives before development
WCAG 2.2 is referenced, while formal accessibility obligations require situation-specific assessment
Core Web Vitals are part of page experience and good scores do not guarantee top rankings
The legal and privacy section is a working checklist rather than legal advice
Belgian company information and cookie guidance is explicitly presented as Belgian context
A sitemap does not guarantee crawling or indexing
noindex is not a security measure
Old URLs should redirect to the most relevant new destination, not automatically to the homepage
Launch is followed by ownership, maintenance, measurement and review
